gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 reviewed the live-server ownership inversion and found one P2 compatibility issue: legacy callers patching implementation globals such as restaurant_kv_serving.live_server.OpenAICompatibleCompletionEngine would no longer affect legacy main(). The issue was fixed by bridging all historical legacy compatibility exports except main into document_kv_cache.live_server while legacy main() runs, with regression coverage for a legacy-patched engine class. Re-review found no issues.

issues.resolution: Expanded the legacy main bridge to project all compatibility exports except main into document_kv_cache.live_server during legacy main() execution and added a regression for a legacy-patched OpenAICompatibleCompletionEngine.
issues.summary: Legacy live_server.main() monkeypatch compatibility was incomplete for implementation globals after moving the live-server implementation into the document module.

scope: document and legacy package README migration notes
verification: poetry run pytest tests/test_live_server.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py -q
python -m py_compile src/document_kv_cache/live_server.py src/restaurant_kv_serving/live_server.py tests/test_live_server.py tests/test_public_package.py
find src tests -name '*.py' -print0 | xargs -0 python -m py_compile
GPT-5.5 review found one legacy monkeypatch compatibility issue; the issue was fixed and re-review returned no findings
what_changed: Moved the live OpenAI-compatible smoke-check implementation into document_kv_cache.live_server.
Turned restaurant_kv_serving.live_server into a compatibility wrapper over the document module.
Kept the public document __all__ curated while preserving the legacy wrapper's broader historical import surface.
Preserved legacy main() monkeypatch behavior for implementation globals through a broad compatibility bridge.
Updated package READMEs and public-package tests to record the new implementation boundary.
why: The generalized package should own live serving smoke checks directly because they validate established vLLM/SGLang OpenAI-compatible endpoints for arbitrary documents. This is a behavior-preserving migration step toward retiring restaurant-specific implementation ownership.
